 {"id":"bd-s39o.1","title":"Test and fix database clients under LD_PRELOAD (psql, mysql, sqlite3)","description":"Test psql, mysql/mariadb client, and sqlite3 under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: socket operations, SSL/TLS (via OpenSSL), readline, locale-aware formatting, signal handling.
Run: LD_PRELOAD=... psql --version, sqlite3 --version, mysql --version
Fix any crashes, then test with actual database connections.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:51.900137264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:52.118219604Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:50.809758463Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.1","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:51.900137264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1337,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"sqlite3, psql, mysql not installed on this system. Cannot test. Deferred.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s39o.2","title":"Test and fix build tools under LD_PRELOAD (cmake, ninja, gcc)","description":"Test cmake, ninja, and gcc under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: fork/exec heavily, pipe/waitpid, tempfile creation, stat/lstat trees, environment manipulation.
Start with --version, then test actual build operations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:53.059194748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:29.300141372Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:28.041737230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.2","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:53.059194748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1336,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"gcc/g++ --version now works. Actual compilation via gcc has a -plugin linker argument issue (ld gets wrong args). Tracked separately.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s39o.3","title":"Test and fix network diagnostic tools under LD_PRELOAD (wget, dig, nslookup)","description":"Test wget, dig, nslookup, host under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: DNS resolution (getaddrinfo beyond /etc/hosts), socket connect, SSL/TLS, HTTP parsing.
Our getaddrinfo only supports numeric hosts and /etc/hosts lookups. For DNS resolution, we'd need to either:
1. Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric hosts
2. Implement a DNS resolver stub
Option 1 is more practical for now.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31.400999331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:30.152849132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:16.988805266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"wget resolves DNS, connects, but GnuTLS has thread locking error (separate SSL issue). curl works perfectly including HTTPS. dig/nslookup are statically linked (can't preload). host works.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-thm2","title":"Fix c11threads managed-attr/host-pthread init mismatch","description":"c11threads_abi::mtx_init was building a FrankenLibC-managed pthread mutexattr word and passing it into host pthread_mutex_init, which triggered a glibc __pthread_tpp_change_priority assertion in c11threads_abi_test. Fix the C11 mutex/condvar init lane so C11 synchronization objects are initialized consistently on the native-managed path, then verify the focused C11 suite and workspace ABI checks via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentFrog","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:34:41.394889788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204943932Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204703667Z","close_reason":"Resolved C11 mutex/condvar init mismatch by keeping C11 synchronization initialization on the native-managed lane; focused c11threads tests now pass and workspace tests advance to unrelated dlfcn failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1358,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented scoped native-mutex delegation helpers in pthread_abi and switched c11threads mutex/condvar init to use the native-managed lane. Verification: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test test_mtx_init_lock_unlock_destroy -- --exact --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed; `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed (36/36); `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passed; `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed; `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` completed without diffs. Workspace-wide `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` is still running under rch session 98302.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:36:15Z"},{"id":1359,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Broader verification update: `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets` no longer aborts in c11threads; the next remaining workspace failure is unrelated in `-p frankenlibc-abi --test dlfcn_abi_test` (14 failures, first at `dlerror_consumed_after_read`, then lock poisoning across dlopen/dlsym/dlvsym tests). This confirms the c11threads glibc assertion is resolved.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vfl","title":"Docs: align README/FEATURE_PARITY with support taxonomy (no overclaim)","description":"Critique mapping: #2 + bottom-line.

Deliverables:
- Update README to include an explicit truth table:
  - what this is today (interposition vs replacement)
  - which subsystems are still call-through
  - which are stubs
- Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to reflect the same classification.

Acceptance:
- No claim of full replacement unless supported by the matrix + E2E harness.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:46.293544458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767467115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767444111Z","close_reason":"Aligned README/FEATURE_PARITY to support taxonomy snapshot; removed full-replacement overclaims; added hybrid interposition truth table","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs"]}
 {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

## Why
End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.
- Thresholds catch >X% regressions (configurable) in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.833286241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48.417613055Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:39.911611946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1147,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"BrightMoose","text":"Added per-kernel microbench binary crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs (machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines) and wired it in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml.

Also hardened scripts/perf_gate.sh for dev environments:
- skip under extreme system load (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_SKIP_OVERLOADED=1, GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR=0.85)
- optional kernel-suite run flag (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1) collecting RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines.

Baseline comparisons for kernel-suite metrics not yet enforced (needs baseline schema + stable host load to record p50s).","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:32:28Z"},{"id":1148,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete by CobaltCompass. BrightMoose delivered per-kernel bench with 6 criterion groups: risk_upper_bound_ppm, bandit_select_profile, barrier_admissible, pareto_recommend_profile, design_choose_plan, approachability_observe/summary. Machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines for CI gating. Wired into Cargo.toml and perf_gate.sh (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1). Note: per-kernel baseline enforcement deferred to stable host load recording.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-zh1y.3.3","title":"Build native stream registry replacing _IO_list_all interop","description":"Replace glibc _IO_list_all linked list interop with our own thread-safe stream registry. Must support: fclose walking all open streams, fflush(NULL) flushing all streams, stdin/stdout/stderr pre-registration. Use a simple mutex-protected linked list or array. This replaces both init_host_io_state and reset_host_io_state functions.","acceptance_criteria":"Stream registry holds all open FILE* in thread-safe list. fflush(NULL) flushes all registered streams. fclose removes from registry. stdin/stdout/stderr pre-registered. Unit tests: open 10 files, fflush(NULL), verify all flushed. Concurrent open/close from 4 threads with no UB.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:34.479603711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:25:36.303202323Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io-internal","native","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:34.479603711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:35.036095116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.3.4","title":"Remove init_host_io_state/reset_host_io_state and all _IO_* host resolution","description":"Once native FILE, vtable, and registry are working: delete init_host_io_state(), reset_host_io_state(), copy_host_object(), bootstrap_host_libio_exports(), and all resolve_host_symbol_raw calls for _IO_list_all, _IO_file_jumps, _IO_wfile_jumps. Update any callers. Add comprehensive tests: fflush(NULL) flushes all, exit flushes all, concurrent fopen/fclose safety. Verify io_internal_abi.rs has zero host delegations. Verify all stdio_abi_test (88) and io_abi_test (39) pass.","acceptance_criteria":"Zero resolve_host_symbol_raw in io_internal_abi.rs. All 88 stdio_abi_test + 39 io_abi_test pass. LD_PRELOAD smoke: echo/cat/ls/sort produce correct output. e2e script passes in both strict and hardened modes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:34.677237376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:25:36.416219423Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["host-delegation","io-internal","native","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:34.677237376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:35.198119436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.4","title":"Promote stdio stdin/stdout/stderr and setvbuf to native FILE (5 delegations)","description":"## Goal
Eliminate 5 host delegations in stdio_abi.rs by owning stdin/stdout/stderr globals and removing host function cache.

## Classification: MIXED
- 3 calls are GENUINELY MISSING (stdin/stdout/stderr global resolution — Category B)
- 2 calls are INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE (setvbuf + host fn cache for non-registry FILE* — Category A)

### Current code structure (verified by audit)
| Line | Symbol | Function | Category |
|------|--------|----------|----------|
| 328 | dynamic (param) | host_stdio_symbol() cache | A — interpose |
| 587 | _IO_2_1_stdin_ | init_host_stdio_streams() | B — missing |
| 588 | _IO_2_1_stdout_ | init_host_stdio_streams() | B — missing |
| 589 | _IO_2_1_stderr_ | init_host_stdio_streams() | B — missing |
| 2001 | setvbuf | setvbuf() | A — interpose |

### Why stdin/stdout/stderr resolve from host
Currently, FrankenLibC's exported stdin/stdout/stderr pointers are set to glibc's internal _IO_2_1_stdin_ etc. This makes them real FILE* that glibc functions can operate on. Without this, any glibc function that touches stdin/stdout/stderr would crash.

### Implementation plan
BLOCKED BY bd-zh1y.3 (native FILE struct). Once we have native FILE:
1. Create static FrankenLibC-owned FILE structs for fd 0/1/2
2. Initialize buffer state: stderr=unbuffered, stdout=line-buffered if isatty(1), stdin=full-buffered
3. Export these as stdin/stdout/stderr globals
4. Native setvbuf: set buffer mode/size on our FILE struct
5. Remove host_stdio_symbol cache (no longer needed)

### Coordination
JadeOtter is currently working on stdio_abi.rs (bd-2vv.3.2 printf family). Coordinate to avoid conflicts.

## Testing requirements
- Unit tests: stdin/stdout/stderr are non-null at program start
- Unit tests: fprintf(stdout, ...) produces output to fd 1
- Unit tests: setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0) makes stdout unbuffered
- Unit tests: setvbuf(stdout, buf, _IOFBF, 4096) switches to full buffering
- Unit tests: line-buffered stdout flushes on \n
- e2e script: scripts/check_native_stdio_globals.sh
  - Verify echo/printf/puts produce correct output under LD_PRELOAD
  - Verify stderr output appears immediately (unbuffered)
  - Compare output of 10+ common programs with and without LD_PRELOAD
- Structured log fields: trace_id, api_family=stdio, symbol, stream (stdin/stdout/stderr/other), buffer_mode
- Verify: all stdio_abi_test tests pass
- Verify: zero resolve_host_symbol_raw in stdio_abi.rs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.459519693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:24:34.263560492Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["host-delegation","native","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.4","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.459519693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.4","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:57.879244926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-zh1y.5","title":"Classify and minimize dlfcn host delegations for interpose model (4 calls)","description":"## Goal
Classify and document 4 dlfcn host delegations; eliminate the 1 genuinely missing (bootstrap dlopen).

## Classification: MOSTLY INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE
- 1 call is GENUINELY MISSING (dlopen bootstrap passthrough — Category B)
- 3 calls are INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE (dlsym/dlclose/dlopen for host-loaded handles — Category A)

### Current code structure (verified by audit)
| Line | Symbol | Function | Category | Condition |
|------|--------|----------|----------|-----------|
| 169 | dlopen | dlopen() bootstrap | B — missing | UNCONDITIONAL during bootstrap |
| 223 | dlopen | dlopen() runtime | A — interpose | non-native filename |
| 281 | dlsym | dlsym() | A — interpose | non-native handle |
| 422 | dlclose | dlclose() | A — interpose | non-main-program handle |

### Why most dlfcn delegations MUST remain for interpose
When a program calls dlopen("libfoo.so"), the host dynamic linker (ld-linux.so) must handle it because it owns the link map, RPATH/RUNPATH resolution, symbol versioning, IFUNC resolution, and TLS allocation for loaded libraries. FrankenLibC cannot reimplement ld-linux.so in the interpose model. Similarly, dlsym/dlclose on host-opened handles must go through the host.

### What CAN be eliminated
The bootstrap dlopen (line 169) is called during early init when BOOTSTRAP_PASSTHROUGH is active. This can be replaced by deferring the open or using our own init-time mechanism.

### Implementation plan
1. Eliminate bootstrap dlopen passthrough by deferring library loads past bootstrap phase
2. For Category A delegations: add IntentionalHostDelegation annotation in support_matrix
3. Add a FORCE_NATIVE_DLFCN flag (like FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING) that errors on host delegation — useful for testing the replace model path
4. Document that full elimination requires a native ELF loader (epic bd-1gg)

## Testing requirements
- Unit tests: dlopen(NULL)/dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT, "printf") resolves our symbols natively
- Unit tests: dlclose on native handles is a no-op (returns 0)
- Unit tests: dlerror returns appropriate messages for invalid handles
- e2e script: scripts/check_dlfcn_native.sh
  - Test dlopen(NULL) + dlsym for known FrankenLibC symbols
  - Test dlopen("libc.so.6") returns a handle (host delegation path)
  - Test dlsym on host handle finds host symbols
  - Verify no crash on dlclose of host handles
- Structured log fields: trace_id, api_family=dlfcn, symbol, handle_type (native/host), resolution_path
- Verify: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test dlfcn_abi_test (21 tests pass)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.599832549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:24:43.464750950Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dlfcn","host-delegation","loader"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.5","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.599832549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-zh1y.5","title":"Classify and minimize dlfcn host delegations for interpose model (4 calls)","description":"## Goal
Classify and document 4 dlfcn host delegations; eliminate the 1 genuinely missing (bootstrap dlopen).

## Classification: MOSTLY INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE
- 1 call is GENUINELY MISSING (dlopen bootstrap passthrough — Category B)
- 3 calls are INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE (dlsym/dlclose/dlopen for host-loaded handles — Category A)

### Current code structure (verified by audit)
| Line | Symbol | Function | Category | Condition |
|------|--------|----------|----------|-----------|
| 169 | dlopen | dlopen() bootstrap | B — missing | UNCONDITIONAL during bootstrap |
| 223 | dlopen | dlopen() runtime | A — interpose | non-native filename |
| 281 | dlsym | dlsym() | A — interpose | non-native handle |
| 422 | dlclose | dlclose() | A — interpose | non-main-program handle |

### Why most dlfcn delegations MUST remain for interpose
When a program calls dlopen("libfoo.so"), the host dynamic linker (ld-linux.so) must handle it because it owns the link map, RPATH/RUNPATH resolution, symbol versioning, IFUNC resolution, and TLS allocation for loaded libraries. FrankenLibC cannot reimplement ld-linux.so in the interpose model. Similarly, dlsym/dlclose on host-opened handles must go through the host.

### What CAN be eliminated
The bootstrap dlopen (line 169) is called during early init when BOOTSTRAP_PASSTHROUGH is active. This can be replaced by deferring the open or using our own init-time mechanism.

### Implementation plan
1. Eliminate bootstrap dlopen passthrough by deferring library loads past bootstrap phase
2. For Category A delegations: add IntentionalHostDelegation annotation in support_matrix
3. Add a FORCE_NATIVE_DLFCN flag (like FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING) that errors on host delegation — useful for testing the replace model path
4. Document that full elimination requires a native ELF loader (epic bd-1gg)

## Testing requirements
- Unit tests: dlopen(NULL)/dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT, "printf") resolves our symbols natively
- Unit tests: dlclose on native handles is a no-op (returns 0)
- Unit tests: dlerror returns appropriate messages for invalid handles
- e2e script: scripts/check_dlfcn_native.sh
  - Test dlopen(NULL) + dlsym for known FrankenLibC symbols
  - Test dlopen("libc.so.6") returns a handle (host delegation path)
  - Test dlsym on host handle finds host symbols
  - Verify no crash on dlclose of host handles
- Structured log fields: trace_id, api_family=dlfcn, symbol, handle_type (native/host), resolution_path
- Verify: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test dlfcn_abi_test (21 tests pass)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentFrog","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.599832549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:53:54.661037009Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dlfcn","host-delegation","loader"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.5","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.599832549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.6","title":"Own process environ array natively for setenv/unsetenv/putenv (3 delegations)","description":"## Goal
Eliminate 3 host delegations in stdlib_abi.rs by natively owning the process environ array.

## Classification: INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE (but promotable)
All 3 delegations PREFER host but have working native fallbacks (native_setenv_sym, native_unsetenv_sym, native_putenv_sym). The host preference exists to avoid environ ownership conflicts, not because native implementation is missing.

### Current code structure (verified by audit)
| Line | Symbol | Function | Fallback |
|------|--------|----------|----------|
| 69 | setenv | native_setenv() | native_setenv_sym() — direct environ manipulation |
| 96 | unsetenv | native_unsetenv() | native_unsetenv_sym() — scan+shift environ |
| 1370 | putenv | putenv() | native_putenv_sym() — store string in environ |

### Why host is currently preferred
The environ array is allocated by glibc's crt0 startup code. If FrankenLibC reallocs it with our allocator, glibc code that later tries to manipulate environ (e.g., internal setlocale) would use glibc's allocator on our memory, causing heap corruption. The reentry guards (NATIVE_SETENV_REENTRY atomics) prevent infinite recursion.

### Implementation plan
Phase 1: Environ ownership transfer at startup
- During FrankenLibC init (__libc_start_main), deep-copy the environ array into our own allocation
- Replace the global environ pointer with our copy
- Track that we own environ so setenv/unsetenv can safely realloc

Phase 2: Switch to native-first
- Remove the resolve_host_symbol_raw preference path
- Always use native_setenv_sym/native_unsetenv_sym/native_putenv_sym
- Keep reentry guards for defense-in-depth

Phase 3: Thread safety hardening
- Add process-wide RwLock for environ mutations (writer for set/unset, reader for getenv)
- This prevents TOCTOU races that exist in the current implementation

### Risks
- Programs that store pointers to environ entries (via putenv) depend on the string remaining valid — must not free putenv'd strings
- Some programs access environ directly (not through getenv) — our copy must be the same global
- Startup ordering: must transfer ownership before any setenv/getenv calls
- clearenv() must also work with our owned array

## Testing requirements
- Unit tests: setenv/getenv round-trip, overwrite=0 vs overwrite=1
- Unit tests: unsetenv removes variable, getenv returns NULL after
- Unit tests: putenv stores the string itself (not a copy), getenv sees it
- Unit tests: concurrent setenv from multiple threads (no corruption)
- Unit tests: environ array is NULL-terminated after mutations
- Unit tests: clearenv() empties the array, getenv returns NULL for all
- e2e script: scripts/check_environ_native.sh
  - Run env under LD_PRELOAD, verify environ is populated
  - Run a C test program that does setenv+getenv+unsetenv+putenv cycle
  - Verify no memory corruption (run under ASAN if available)
- Structured log fields: trace_id, api_family=stdlib, symbol, environ_size, key, action (set/unset/put)
- Verify: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdlib_abi_test (107 tests pass)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.740448478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:24:34.489548469Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["environ","host-delegation","native","stdlib"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.6","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.740448478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
